108.status: Faced hitters for the first time on April 7, throwing 26 pitches. Will meet with the training staff on April 8 to determine if he's ready to start a rehab assignment.
Feeling good playing catch and doing strengthening exercises. Said on April 7 that he expects to throw his first bullpen within a week and that he's been making steady progress.
Scratched from his start due to illness on April 5 and was placed on the IL on April 6. Expected to be ready to return once he's eligible on April 18.

Began a rehab assignment and played third base for Triple-A Salt Lake on March 31. Has hit .429 in six games in his rehab assignment with Triple-A Salt Lake and is getting closer to a return, manager Kurt Suzuki said April 6.
Right middle fingernail fell off after March 17 start. Has progressed to throwing bullpens with all of his pitches at the club's complex in Arizona.

Progressing well in throwing program. Yet to throw bullpens at 100 percent intensity but has been throwing without any issues. Manager Kurt Suzuki said he looks good but doesn't know when he'll face hitters just yet.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and will have to determine his next step, general manager Perry Minasian said. There's at least a chance Stephenson could require another surgery.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to discomfort, then went on IL. Underwent imaging on April 1. Getting second opinion from Dr. Keith Meister on April 7.
Bone removed from right hand on Feb. 12. Played six rehab games for Triple-A Norfolk from March 27-April 5, going 5-for-23 (.217) with two doubles and three RBIs.
After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20. Swinging a bat as of April 1. Scheduled to begin throwing progression week of April 6.
Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Norfolk on April 1, allowing one run in two-thirds of an inning. Pitched a scoreless inning with two strikeouts in second appearance on April 5.
Allowed one run in one inning at Pittsburgh on April 3, then did not pitch in final two games of series. Went on IL prior to April 6 game vs. White Sox in Chicago.
Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing vs. Rangers. Went on IL five days later.
Felt discomfort while warming up in bullpen on March 21. Underwent imaging in Baltimore on March 23. Playing catch and running as of March 29.
Sustained injury during March 19 Grapefruit League game. Underwent imaging in Baltimore on March 23. Placed on IL to begin season.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return yet.

111.status: Will start Minor League rehab assignment for Triple-A Worcester on April 11.
Tried to resume swinging but is still experiencing discomfort in his ribs.
Will start rehab assignment with Triple-A Worcester on April 8.
Red Sox awaiting results of second opinion with Dr. Keith Meister on April 7.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Started gradual throwing program on flat ground in the second week of Spring Training.

112.status: Will undergo season-ending right elbow surgery, manager Craig Counsell announced on April 7.
Has felt biceps soreness for a while and wasn't bouncing back after starts as well as he liked. Not expected to need more than a minimum stay on the IL.
Played with Double-A Knoxville on April 3-5 and will do so again on April 7-8, per Counsell. Barring anything unexpected, Suzuki will rejoin the Cubs for the April 10-12 home series vs. Pittsburgh.
Shut down from throwing on March 8. After a period of rest and rehab, resumed playing catch on April 4. Still "weeks" away, per manager Craig Counsell.
Resumed playing catch on March 3 after 10-day shutdown. Moved back to mound March 16 and was facing hitters as of April 5. Still likely "weeks" away, per manager Craig Counsell.

Medically cleared Feb. 28 by Dr. Keith Meister, who performed Steele's elbow surgery (left ulnar collateral ligament revision repair) on April 18, 2025. Has advanced to facing hitters in live BP sessions.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Cubs manager Craig Counsell said Austin could miss "months" while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Lodolo resumed playing catch April 5 but with a Plyo ball without seams so his finger can continue to heal. His rehab assignment start at Single-A Daytona on April 2 was cut short at 40 pitches because the blister returned.
Threw on flat ground from 90 feet on April 1. Ferguson will travel with the club on its first road trip.
Had arthroscopic surgery March 11 to remove bone chips and loose bodies from elbow. Shut down from throwing for six weeks; expected to miss 14-16 weeks.

114.status: Suffered injury running to second base while hitting a double in fifth inning on April 6. Imaging revealed moderate strain. Next steps and return-to-play timeline to be determined.
Set to make fourth appearance of rehab assignment with Triple-A Columbus on April 7.
Continuing rehab assignment with Triple-A Columbus. Building up to physically handle playing five to six days per week.
Threw simulated game in Arizona in most recent step of build-up. Continuing to progress well.

115.status: Said on April 5 that the injury was feeling better and he has been playing catch, but he is waiting to be cleared for more extensive activity.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Had been facing hitters, but now has backed off to playing catch.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
Targeting the middle of April to return to Minor League action.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Completing a scheduled rest period as part of his rehab program after playing catch out to 120 feet over the past few weeks.
Felt shoulder discomfort after throwing during the week of April 1. Undergoing further evaluation.
Scheduled to begin a rehab assignment with Single-A Lakeland on April 7.
Placed on 7-day injured list at Triple-A Toledo on April 5.
Scratched from his April 5 start -- what was to be his first start in a Tigers home uniform since Aug. 20, 2017 -- one day prior. Threw a bullpen session on April 6.
Completing a return-to-play throwing program that began the week of March 30. Sustained a groin strain while doing a routine workout off the mound.
Completing a return-to-play throwing program that began on March 9. Expected to pitch again this season, according to team president of baseball operations Scott Harris.
Underwent surgery on Feb. 2, after offseason rehab program was unsuccessful.

117.status: Will “refrain from throwing for a few weeks,” according to the club, and will be reevaluated in two weeks. Manager Joe Espada that the issue is muscular, with no evidence of damage to ligaments.
Scheduled to throw live batting practice April 7 in West Palm Beach, Fla., then two innings in a simulated game there on April 11.
Threw live batting practice session on April 7 in West Palm Beach, Fla..
Shut down in early February. Is throwing bullpens and could face hitters during the week of April 13.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Threw a light bullpen session April 6, although pain symptoms haven't disappeared completely.
Began throwing bullpen sessions in mid-March. Threw three innings (49 pitches) April 2 in extended spring camp.
Suffered minor setback while in extended spring camp, but is scheduled to start throwing again April 6.
Has been battling valgus extension overload (VEO) in left elbow, experiencing pain when extending. Received injection and will be shut down for next few days.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Was originally in the lineup on April 7, but was scratched about 20 minutes before first pitch to tend to a family matter.
An MRI revealed a right oblique strain. Both Betts and manager Dave Roberts are hopeful that he could return before the typical timeline of 4-6 weeks.
Throwing bullpen sessions. He is scheduled to begin facing hitters during the week of April 6.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in Stone's shoulder. He has started a throwing program and essentially has an entire Spring Training ramp-up ahead.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Faced hitters for the first time on March 27 and will throw at least one more live session before beginning a rehab assignment.
Has not thrown since March 13, when he tweaked his side while throwing in the bullpen before a spring start. He will not begin a throwing program until he's pain-free.
Dealt with shoulder soreness during Spring Training. He progressed to throwing off the mound by the time the Dodgers broke camp, but the team is building him up slowly.
Velocity is down from where it is expected to be, so the Dodgers are slow-playing him. He is ramping up to greater distances in his catch play.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025. He started throwing full bullpen sessions in mid-March.

120.status: Continuing rehab but remains shut down from throwing. Felt soreness pitching on March 20. Nats exercising caution because Gray is returning from Tommy John surgery.
Continuing to progress with throwing program; threw a pair of 20-pitch bullpen sessions on March 31 and April 3.
Continuing throwing progression and playing catch.

120.playerName: Jarlin Susana (Nationals' No. 4 prospect, MLB No. 80)
120.status: Threw 15 pitches at a distance of 60 feet on Feb. 20; continuing throwing progressing, with flat ground throwing three times a week.

120.playerName: Travis Sykora (Nationals' No. 2 prospect, MLB No. 54)
120.status: Continuing throwing progression, including throwing on flat ground three times a week.

121.status: Strained his calf running first to third in an April 3 game in San Francisco. Is expected to miss 2-3 weeks.
Jammed his thumb on a headfirst slide on April 4. Was initially in the lineup and playing right field April 5, but was scratched pregame. Should be available to pinch-hit.
Is about a month behind other Mets pitchers. Will spend early April in Port St. Lucie rehabbing and pitching in camp games before advancing to an affiliate.
Exited the Mets' March 21 game after four innings due to knee discomfort and underwent an MRI, which revealed the tear. Will spend his recovery on the Minor League IL.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: After originally rehabbing from a lumbar spine strain that sidelined him through Spring Training, Hoglund experienced symptoms related to a left hip impingement and is shut down while undergoing further evaluation.

134.status: General manager Ben Cherington didn't provide a specific timeline for Triolo when asked of his return but said it would be a matter of weeks, not days. Triolo was injured while running the bases in the team’s home-opener on April 3.
Pitched a live batting practice on April 6 that went well and showcased all of his pitches, per general manager Ben Cherington.

135.status: Has made three scoreless rehab appearances, including on back-to-back days April 4 and 5. "Getting closer," said manager Craig Stammen.
Pitched 2 1/3 innings for Triple-A El Paso on April 4, his first game action since June 2025. Expected to require a lengthy buildup, akin to a full Spring Training.
Missed the World Baseball Classic due to the injury, then began the season on the IL. Began a rehab stint with Triple-A El Paso on April 4, pitching a scoreless inning.
Wasn't built up enough to make the Opening Day roster. Has made scoreless starts of three and four innings, respectively, with Triple-A El Paso while on a rehab assignment.
Sustained the injury during the offseason, then aggravated it during a game on March 5. Began a rehab stint with Triple-A El Paso on March 27.
Made one spring start, but experienced soreness afterward and was sidelined. Has resumed playing catch, but has yet to throw off a mound.
Only appeared in five Cactus League games after sustaining the injury in early March. By the end of camp, had begun doing light drills.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. Resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp.

136.status: Suffered injury during catch play leading up to Opening Day. Team does not have timetable on return, though lat issues typically require rest, recovery and rehab.More »

Threw 30-pitch bullpen session with no issues on March 17. Manager Dan Wilson said Miller will throw “maybe another couple bullpen sessions where he’s not feeling it, and we’ll kind of go from there.”More »
Hurt his calf playing for Team Italy at World Baseball Classic. Wasn't ready to return to play by Opening Day
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.

137.status: Scheduled to begin a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on April 7.
Began a throwing program on April 6 in Arizona. Currently throwing off flat ground.
Scheduled to throw live batting practice on April 7 in Arizona.
Scratched from the Giants' starting lineup on April 3 but could return to action early the week of April 6.
Diagnosed with a blood clot in his right arm and underwent surgery to have it removed on April 3. Expected to miss two to four months.
Underwent surgery to repair the anterior capsule in his right shoulder in May 2025. Started a bullpen progression on Feb. 10 and is throwing twice per week.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025. Was slated to begin a throwing progression in mid-March.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Memphis on March 31. Pitched five innings, allowing three runs on 70 pitches vs. Norfolk.
Pushard felt right knee soreness on March 30, per manager Oliver Marmol. After imaging, the team believed he would be unavailable for several days, triggering the move.
Underwent surgery last October to address Haglund’s deformities in both heels. Started his running progression on March 20, but no date has been set for a return to the lineup.

139.status: Threw a bullpen on March 23 and received an injection on March 25. Threw a bullpen on April 7. Will progress to facing hitters in live BP and likely go on a rehab assignment.
“Didn’t feel great” when playing catch Feb. 19. Received a cortisone injection on Feb. 20. Joined Double-A Montgomery to begin a rehab assignment on April 7.

Served as DH (0-for-3, walk) on April 5 for Triple-A Durham, then traveled with the Bulls to Scranton/Wilkes-Barre, where he’s expected to play second base. “So far, everything’s gone well,” manager Kevin Cash said.
Manager Kevin Cash said Cleavinger will get a couple of days off before resuming a throwing program. “I'd like to be optimistic right now that he'll be back shorter than later,” Cash added.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Will resume throwing around April 6, but will need a while to build up after being shut down so long.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July. Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 10.

140.status: Suffered an intercostal strain during a perfect inning of relief on April 4. His outing ended prematurely, as he should have gone another frame. Manager Skip Schumaker said he doesn’t expect the IL stint to last too long.

Threw 27 pitches in his first rehab assignment with Triple-A Round Rock on April 2, allowing a pair of solo homers in his two innings. Manager Skip Schumaker said he came out of it “feeling good” as he continues to progress.
Down four to six weeks minimum before re-evaluation.
Took a comebacker off the bat of Kyle Higashioka in batting practice and will be out for about four weeks.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: Underwent surgery on April 7 and had a screw inserted in his thumb. Expectation is a six-week recovery.
Will undergo surgery on April 15 and is expected to miss six months, which likely ends his season.
Left after two innings and 36 pitches April 6. Both Scherzer and John Schneider believe he’ll make his next start.
Will pitch again for Single-A Dunedin on April 9, targeting four innings and roughly 60 pitches.
Felt good coming out of a recent bullpen session and will throw again April 7.
Continues to throw bullpen sessions and will soon face live hitters one to two times before moving into rehab games.
Felt good coming out of a live BP session over the weekend and will throw another bullpen April 7.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

Was scheduled to begin throwing again March 10, which is a step in the right direction after being shut down the past couple of weeks. The Blue Jays were working to find some preventative measures to help Tiedemann avoid further elbow soreness in the future.

142.status: Began rehabilitation assignment at Single-A Fort Myers on April 4.
Is progressing in long toss program, expected to throw off a mound in mid-April.
Will throw a live batting practice this week and could progress to a rehabilitation assignment after that.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.More >

143.status: Did not start on April 7 in San Francisco after leaving the series opener with a “little bit” of groin tightness. Could return to the lineup for the April 8 series finale.

Threw three innings in first rehab start with Triple-A Lehigh Valley on March 28. Threw three more in a second Triple-A outing on April 3, and will make another rehab start on April 8. On track for April return.

143.playerName: Aidan Miller (Phillies' No. 1 prospect, No. 21 overall)
143.status: Manager Rob Thomson said Miller’s “activities have increased,” but he still has not progressed enough from his lingering back injury to start swinging a bat.
Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique. Phillies manager Rob Thomson said it’s “very mild.”

144.status: Threw a bullpen session on April 7. Next step will be live batting practice before beginning a rehab assignment.
Begins a rehab assignment with Gwinnett on April 10.

144.injury: Torn tendon in right middle finger
144.status: Still hasn't faced live pitching, but the Braves are hoping he could begin a rehab assignment within the next couple weeks.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow. Could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. There is a chance he could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Manager Will Venable listed the injury, suffered on April 6, as a Grade 1 strain when talking on April 7. Hays will be out of action for 2 to 4 weeks.
According to the White Sox, Baldwin elected to undergo a season-ending UCL internal brace procedure on April 1. He is expected to be ready by Spring Training.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but he was with the team for the home opener. Transferred to the 60-day IL on April 7.
Sustained the injury on April 1 after going 4-for-16 with one home run to start the season. He spoke on April 7 of not being 100 percent but was getting close.
Feeling much better and will begin hitting off a tee around April 7 or 8. A Minor League rehab assignment is in sight as he continues to progress.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.
Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.

146.playerName: Aiva Arquette (Marlins' No. 4 prospect, No. 45 overall)
146.status: Underwent core muscle surgery on Feb. 27. Sustained left groin injury during offseason workouts. Rested before arriving in Jupiter, where it flared up again.

According to manager Clayton McCullough on April 6, the club will "see how these next couple days go and then decide when and what a rehab assignment will look like." Felt tightness on March 20 rounding third base. Missed 1-2 weeks with a strain of the same hamstring earlier this spring.
Timeline of 4-6 weeks after being placed on IL on March 28 (retroactive to March 25). Scratched from March 27 lineup.
Placed on 10-day injured list on March 25 (retroactive to March 22). 6-8 week timeline from March 22. Replaced during third-inning at-bat after fouling off an 89.6 mph cutter.

Placed on 10-day injured list on March 25 (retroactive to March 22). Imaging revealed severity after being scratched from March 11 lineup. Stretched his side making a backhanded play during individual pregame work that day.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Placed on 60-day injured list on March 25. Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 12. Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Experiencing right hamstring tightness but continuing to throw, though fielding/covering bases is affected. Tossed bullpen and performed agility work April 4 in New York. Next step is another side session, then possibly a rehab start.
Will throw in game situations and eventually in the Minors over the next several weeks. Threw one "up" of live BP April 1. Tossed bullpen April 4 in New York.
Began taking live batting practice April 1. Could begin a Minor League rehab assignment in the northeast sometime in mid-April.
Spent most of Spring Training throwing on flat ground. Tentatively scheduled to begin throwing bullpen sessions in late March. Could face hitters in April or May.

158.status: Details were murky, but manager Pat Murphy indicated on April 7 that Turang had been playing in pain for the past week or so. (last updated: April 7)
Scheduled for follow-up imaging on April 9. (last updated: April 7)
Felt discomfort while facing hitters in Arizona for the first time, according to an April 7 update from manager Pat Murphy. The schedule calls for another live BP on April 9. (last updated: April 7)
Underwent surgery on March 30 and was scheduled for a follow-up with the surgeon on April 9. (last updated April 7)

Returned from his rehab assignment on April 4 after suffering a rib-cage injury while coming back from a shoulder injury. The setback is expected to delay his comeback by several weeks. (last updated: April 7)
Initial hope is that Koenig can avoid surgery and recover with treatment. (last updated: April 7)
First live batting practice session scheduled for April 8. (last updated: April 7)
Injured running to first base on March 9, and an MRI revealed a more serious injury than initially believed. (last updated: March 25)
A non-roster invitee to big league camp, Garabito had a bone fragment removed and a tendon repaired in his foot. He's expected to miss about four months. (last updated: Feb. 24)
A former top prospect who was in camp as a non-roster invitee, Garcia was injured playing winter ball. (last updated: Feb. 24)
